perturbation.original_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to each Party, such efforts and commitment of resources in accordance with [***] that such Party [***]. As used in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts", "reasonable" shall be measured by [***]. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.changed_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to each Party, such efforts and commitment of resources in accordance with the Party's internal business practices, regardless of industry standards, that such Party deems prudent. As used in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts", "reasonable" shall be measured by the Party’s own assessment of feasibility and cost-effectiveness. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" references an external, potentially objective standard ([***]), while the modified definition makes it entirely dependent on the Party's internal business practices and subjective assessment. This change creates a contradiction because other sections relying on an industry-standard interpretation of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' now become ambiguous. If Antares doesn't replenish the safety stock within [***] of receipt of a Firm Order, as stated in Section 3.6(d), AMAG can now have a hard time proving if they made commercially reasonable efforts to replenish it, because now AMAG's understanding of Commercially Reasonable Efforts, is different in other sections, from this section where the perturbation was inserted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Antares or its Subcontractor shall manage the Safety Stock as part of its overall inventory and use the Safety Stock to fulfill its obligations pursuant to a Firm Orders on a first in/first out basis. As such inventory of Safety Stock is used as part of the Manufacturing Services of Product and/or sample Product, Antares shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to replenish the Safety Stock to the level set forth in Section 3.6(a)(i) (as adjusted pursuant to Section 3.6(a)(ii)) within [***] of receipt of such Firm Order.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' shifts from an objective standard to a subjective, party-specific one, directly contradicting the requirement in Section 3.6(d) that Antares use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to replenish safety stock. This creates ambiguity and potential unenforceability because the standard for 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' is now dependent on Antares's internal practices, making it difficult to assess whether Antares fulfilled its obligation, satisfying the conditions for 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Antares shall, at the Party's respective costs set forth on Exhibit I, develop, implement and maintain an the redundancy plan for molds, tooling and assemblies for the manufacturing of the Devices set forth on Exhibit I.
perturbation.changed_text: Antares shall, at Antares' sole cost, develop, implement, and maintain the redundancy plan for molds, tooling, and assemblies for the manufacturing of the Devices set forth on Exhibit I.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the costs for the redundancy plan are shared as per Exhibit I. Now, the burden is shifted solely to Antares. This contradicts the expectation from Exhibit I and potentially other sections that might reference cost-sharing, creating an internal contradiction regarding financial responsibilities. This contradicts the financial responsibilities set forth on Exhibit I of Antares and AMAG, to develop, implement and maintain the redundacy plan. Now if AMAG doesn't incur on that financial responsibility in developing and maintaining the redundancy plan, Antares can't take any legal action because in this section it says it is solely responseble to incur that financial responsibility.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that costs for the redundancy plan are shared according to Exhibit I. The changed text shifts the entire cost burden to Antares. This directly contradicts the cost-sharing arrangement outlined in Exhibit I, creating a clear financial inconsistency within the contract regarding responsibility for the redundancy plan. This fulfills the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as the responsibilities are inconsistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to early termination of this Agreement pursuant to Sections 7.2, 7.3 or 7.4, this Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue until the expiration or earlier termination of the Development and License Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to early termination of this Agreement pursuant to Sections 7.2, 7.3 or 7.4, this Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years, with automatic renewal for successive one (1) year periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least six (6) months prior to the end of the then-current term (the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: The original term is tied to the Development and License Agreement. The modified term introduces a fixed period with automatic renewals. This change creates a contradiction because other sections that rely on the Development and License Agreement's term to define this agreement's duration will now be unclear. This change contradicts Section 7.4, which states: Without further action by either Party, this Agreement shall automatically terminate effective immediately upon the termination of the Development and License Agreement in its entirety, subject to the provisions that expressly survive the termination thereof. Now if the Development and License Agreement terminates, it will create a contradiction on when does the Manufacturing Agreement terminates
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without further action by either Party, this Agreement shall automatically terminate effective immediately upon the termination of the Development and License Agreement in its entirety, subject to the provisions that expressly survive the termination thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a fixed term with automatic renewals, which directly conflicts with the original clause stating that the agreement terminates automatically upon termination of the Development and License Agreement. The 'Changed Text' now defines the term with a fixed period while the 'Contradicted Text' defines it based on the Development and License Agreement. This presents a clear in-text contradiction that would lead to uncertainty about the agreement's duration, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation.
